title: Vida cotidiana · Food, colours and places
focus: Common food items, colours, simple numbers / quantifiers, places, and very common verbs not yet in group 1.

words.example_en: I buy fresh bread every morning.

words.example: La leche está en la nevera.
words.example_en: The milk is in the fridge.

words.example: Como una manzana en el recreo.
words.example_en: I eat an apple at break.
I love manchego cheese.

words.example: Madrid es una ciudad muy viva.
words.example_en: Madrid is a very lively city.
My street is very quiet.

words.example: Hay una tienda nueva en la esquina.
words.example_en: There's a new shop on the corner.
We're going to the park by car.
I buy fruit on Saturdays.
I watch a film every Friday.

words.example: No oigo bien con tanto ruido.
words.example_en: I can't hear well with so much noise.

words.example: Leo en español todas las noches.
words.example_en: I read in Spanish every night.
I write postcards when I travel.
The red bag is new.
The sky is blue today.
I bought a green shirt.

words.example: Tengo un gato negro y blanco.
words.example_en: I have a black and white cat.
My phone is new.

words.example: Tengo un coche viejo pero fiable.
words.example_en: I have an old but reliable car.

words.example: Aquí hace fresco por la tarde.
words.example_en: It gets cool here in the afternoon.
My friend works there.
Good morning, what would you like?
